title: What should I ask Luis Garicano? - Marginal REVOLUTION
gist: Tyler Cowen announces an upcoming Conversations with Tyler episode with Spanish economist and former MEP Luis Garicano, whose new book Messy Jobs is forthcoming.

insights.claim: Tyler Cowen is preparing to record a Conversations with Tyler episode with Spanish economist and former European Parliament member Luis Garicano.
insights.speaker: Tyler Cowen
insights.so_what: Signals upcoming public commentary from a leading European economic-liberal policymaker on EU economic policy, worth watching once the interview airs.
insights.locator: Yes I will be doing a Conversation with him

insights.claim: Luis Garicano was a Member of the European Parliament from 2019 to 2022 and served as vice president of the Renew Europe group and of the Alliance of Liberals and Democrats for Europe (ALDE) party.
insights.speaker: Tyler Cowen
entities: Alliance of Liberals and Democrats for Europe
insights.so_what: Establishes Garicano's direct policymaking track record inside EU institutions, relevant context for assessing his commentary's influence.
insights.locator: was a Member of the European Parliament

insights.claim: After leaving the European Parliament, Garicano returned to academia, holding visiting positions at Columbia Business School and the University of Chicago Booth School of Business before returning to the London School of Economics in 2023 as a full professor at the School of Public Policy.
insights.speaker: Tyler Cowen
entities: University of Chicago Booth School of Business
insights.so_what: Confirms Garicano's academic credibility spans top US and UK institutions, adding weight to his policy views.

insights.claim: Garicano is described as one of Europe's leading economic liberals.
insights.speaker: Tyler Cowen
insights.so_what: Positions Garicano as an influential voice for pro-market policy in European political debates.
insights.locator: He is one of the leading European economic liberals

insights.claim: Garicano has a forthcoming book, Messy Jobs, co-authored with Jim Li and Yanhui Wu.
insights.speaker: Tyler Cowen
insights.so_what: A book release to watch for insight into labor-market/job-structure themes from a prominent economist.
insights.locator: he also has a very good book coming out called Messy Jobs
